name: browser-use
description: Browser automation MCP server - any MCP-compatible AI can control web browsers directly
long_description:
Browser-Use MCP Server exposes browser control primitives via the Model Context Protocol. Any AI that supports MCP (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.) can directly navigate, click, type, scroll, and extract content from web pages. No internal LLM required - the calling AI is the brain.

### Key Features:
- **Universal AI Compatibility**: Works with any MCP-compatible AI client
- **Direct Browser Control**: Navigate, click, type, scroll, and interact with any website
- **Content Extraction**: Extract page text as clean markdown
- **Keyboard & Form Control**: Send keyboard shortcuts, select dropdown options
- **Tab Management**: Handle multiple tabs and complex workflows
- **Vision Capabilities**: Take screenshots for visual understanding
- **Security Features**: Domain restrictions and sandboxed execution

### Use Cases:
- Web scraping and data extraction
- Automated testing and QA
- Research and information gathering
- Form filling and submissions
- Multi-step workflow automation

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL in the current tab or open a new tab

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Click on an element by its index or at specific viewport coordinates

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into an input field identified by its index

tools.name: browser_get_state
tools.description: Get the current page state including all interactive elements with their indices

tools.name: browser_get_html
tools.description: Get the raw HTML of the current page or a specific element

tools.name: browser_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page

tools.name: browser_extract_content
tools.description: Extract the text content of the current page as clean markdown

tools.name: browser_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the page up or down

tools.name: browser_go_back
tools.description: Navigate back to the previous page in browser history

tools.name: browser_send_keys
tools.description: Send keyboard keys or shortcuts (Enter, Escape, ctrl+a, etc.)

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select an option from a dropdown/select element

tools.name: browser_get_dropdown_options
tools.description: Get all available options from a dropdown/select element

tools.name: browser_wait
tools.description: Wait for a specified number of seconds

tools.name: browser_list_tabs
tools.description: List all open browser tabs with their URLs and titles

tools.name: browser_switch_tab
tools.description: Switch to a different tab by its index

tools.name: browser_close_tab
tools.description: Close a specific tab by its index

tools.name: browser_get_cookies
tools.description: Get all cookies, optionally filtered by domain

tools.name: browser_set_cookies
tools.description: Set one or more cookies in the browser

tools.name: browser_clear_cookies
tools.description: Clear all cookies or cookies for a specific domain

tools.name: browser_save_storage_state
tools.description: Force save cookies and localStorage to disk

prompts.name: scrape_data
prompts.description: Extract structured data from a website
prompts.text: Use browser_navigate to go to {url}, then use browser_extract_content to get page text. If the page requires interaction, use browser_get_state to find elements and browser_click/browser_type as needed.

prompts.name: fill_form
prompts.description: Fill out and submit a web form
prompts.text: Navigate to {url}, use browser_get_state to identify form fields, then use browser_type to fill in: {field_data}. Finally, click the submit button.

prompts.name: research_topic
prompts.description: Research a topic across multiple websites
prompts.text: Open multiple tabs using browser_navigate with new_tab=true for sites: {sites}. Use browser_extract_content on each to gather information about {topic}. Switch between tabs with browser_switch_tab.

workspace_directory.title: Workspace Directory
workspace_directory.description: Directory to use as workspace for downloads and file operations

headless.title: Headless Mode
headless.description: Run browser without GUI (faster but no visual feedback). Set to false to see the browser in action

stealth.title: Stealth Mode
stealth.description: Use stealth mode to avoid detection by anti-bot systems

user_data_dir.title: User Data Directory
user_data_dir.description: Chrome profile directory for persistent sessions (cookies, localStorage, etc)

disable_security.title: Disable Security
disable_security.description: Disable browser security features (CORS, CSP, etc). Use with caution

deterministic_rendering.title: Deterministic Rendering
deterministic_rendering.description: Force deterministic rendering for consistent screenshots. May break some sites

viewport_expansion.title: Viewport Expansion
viewport_expansion.description: Extra pixels to expand viewport for better LLM context understanding

allowed_domains.title: Allowed Domains
allowed_domains.description: Comma-separated list of domains the browser can access (e.g., 'example.com,*.google.com'). Leave empty for no restrictions

sensitive_data_masking.title: Sensitive Data Masking
sensitive_data_masking.description: Mask sensitive data (passwords, credit cards) in screenshots and logs

default_timeout.title: Default Timeout (ms)
default_timeout.description: Default timeout for browser operations in milliseconds

wait_between_actions.title: Wait Between Actions (seconds)
wait_between_actions.description: Time to wait between browser actions for stability
